channel.title: BUbreakingSectionTwo
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUbreakingSectionTwo, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Leqaa Kordia, Columbia Protester in ICE Custody for Nearly a Year, Suffers Seizure
item.description: Leqaa Kordia, 33, of New Jersey, was hospitalized after hitting her head at a Texas detention center, her lawyer said. She was initially arrested duri...

item.title: Google Gemini boss describes working with founders Larry Page and Sergey Brin to win the AI future
item.description: Demis Hassabis told Fortune it's a return to the "shipping culture" from "the golden era of Google."

item.title: Border Officials Are Said to Have Caused El Paso Closure by Firing Anti-Drone Laser
item.description: People familiar with the episode said the use of the technology was not coordinated with the Federal Aviation Administration. Officials targeted what ...

item.title: Mamdani Reverses Campaign Promise to Expand Rental Assistance
item.description: A rental voucher program costs more than $1 billion. The mayor’s decision to curtail its expansion reflects the clash between his ideology and the rea...

item.title: The 70/30 rule that separates millionaires from everyone else
item.description: "Sexy is how you go broke," David Bach argues, saying millionaires know something others don't: Boring is the real sexy.

item.title: Why 50% stay broke and how one hour a day can change everything
item.description: Next time you go to work, David Bach says, try paying yourself first.

item.title: South Korean Court Rules Against Women in LG Inheritance Lawsuit
item.description: The widow and two daughters of LG’s former chairman sought to invalidate an inheritance agreement, claiming they were deceived into forfeiting their r...

item.title: ‘Fertility president’ Trump has demanded a baby boom, and Stanford researchers have a solution: letting more people work from home, study finds
item.description: “It seems like just such an obvious thing to do,” remote work expert Nick Bloom said. “As an economist, I don't think I've ever seen the more obvious,...

item.title: Ukrainian Olympian Is Disqualified Over Helmet Honoring War Dead
item.description: Olympic officials ruled that Vladyslav Heraskevych’s helmet violated a prohibition on political speech, setting off outrage at the Winter Games in Ita...

item.title: HPE turns finance into the front line of enterprise AI
item.description: HPE’s AI named Alfred is shrinking reporting cycles and rewriting the CFO playbook.

item.title: Tumbler Ridge Shooting: What to Know About the Victims and Suspect
item.description: The police identified an 18-year-old as the suspect believed to have fatally shot eight people in Tumbler Ridge, British Columbia, before killing hers...

item.title: Olympic Figure Skaters Are on Thin Ice Over Music Copyright Rules
item.description: Some Olympic athletes have found themselves caught up in controversies over musical choices before and during one of the biggest competitions of their...

item.title: Across Italy, the Winter Olympic Vibes Are Very Different
item.description: In Milan, the hub of the Winter Games, the atmosphere seems subdued, while the small towns hosting mountain events are seeing enthusiastic crowds.

item.title: From the chaos of snowboard cross, a repeat champ.
item.description: Alessandro Hämmerle of Austria came from behind to claim another gold medal in the men’s event.

item.title: Ukrainian Olympian Is Disqualified Over Helmet Honoring War Dead
item.description: Olympic officials ruled that Vladyslav Heraskevych’s helmet violated a prohibition on political speech, setting off outrage at the Winter Games in Ita...
